sections.title: Library Application Form
sections.instructions: Complete the form below. Choose ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.
sections.transcript:
Staff: Good morning! Welcome to the City Library. How can I help you today?

Applicant: Good morning. I would like to apply for a library membership, please.

Staff: Certainly. Let me get the application form. First, could you tell me your first name?

Applicant: Yes, my first name is John, spelled J-O-H-N.

Staff: Great. And what is your surname?

Applicant: And my last name is Smith, S-M-I-T-H.

Staff: Perfect. We have three types of membership: Individual at thirty dollars a year, Family at fifty dollars, and Student at fifteen dollars. Which one would you prefer?

Applicant: Since I'm only using it for myself, I will go with the Individual Membership, which is thirty dollars a year.

Staff: Excellent. Let me complete the application for you...

questions.instruction: First Name: ______
questions.explanation: Khi nhân viên thư viện hỏi tên, ứng viên trả lời rõ ràng: 'my first name is John, spelled J-O-H-N'. Do đó đáp án là John.

questions.instruction: Surname: ______
questions.explanation: Ứng viên đánh vần họ của mình là Smith: 'my last name is Smith, S-M-I-T-H'. Meo/Trap: khong chon theo keyword nghe thay dau tien. Hay nghe tiep cac correction, contrast va y cuoi cung cua speaker; sau do doi chieu spelling, word form, so it/so nhieu va word limit.

questionGroups.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.instruction: What type of membership does the applicant select?
questions.explanation: Ứng viên chọn đăng ký gói cá nhân (Individual Membership) giá 30 đô la/năm: 'I will go with the Individual Membership, which is thirty dollars a year.' Nên phương án A là đáp án đúng.

questions.explanation: Ngay tại lối vào thư viện là quầy lễ tân đón tiếp độc giả: 'Welcome to the City Library. How can I help you today?' Meo/Trap: khong chon theo keyword nghe thay dau tien. sections.title: New Employee Orientation
sections.instructions: Answer the questions below based on Part 2 of the listening recording.
sections.transcript:
Manager: Good morning everyone and welcome to your orientation week here at GlobalTech. I want to walk you through a few essential house rules and admin requirements.

First, our daily training sessions will kick off at exactly 9:30 AM, so please arrive by 9:20 to settle in.

Second, regarding paperwork: your signed employment contract must be submitted to HR by Friday afternoon at the latest.

Third, parking: the main employee parking lot is located in Sector D of the building complex. You will need a parking permit which you can get today.

To enter the building after today, you must collect your official security badge from the main reception desk in the lobby.

For lunch, you are welcome to visit our great onsite cafeteria, which offers hot meals daily at a discount.

Let's talk about dress code: regular weekdays require a smart casual standard, except for Fridays which are casual.

Performance reviews are carried out semi-annually by your department supervisor.

If you experience any IT access issues today, please call the IT helpdesk team at extension 404.

Regarding mobile phones: you are permitted to make personal calls only during official breaks.

Finally, for safety: evacuation maps are posted near all elevators in the building. Familiarize yourself with them today.

questionGroups.instruction: Complete the orientation notes. Choose ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

questions.explanation: Quản lý thông báo: 'daily training sessions will kick off at exactly 9:30 AM.' Meo/Trap: khong chon theo keyword nghe thay dau tien. Hay nghe tiep cac correction, contrast va y cuoi cung cua speaker; sau do doi chieu spelling, word form, so it/so nhieu va word limit.

sections.title: Academic Climate Change Seminar
sections.instructions: Answer the questions below based on Part 3 of the listening recording.
sections.transcript:
Professor: Good afternoon. Let's begin our seminar on modern climate issues. Today, we're reviewing Dr. Green's latest research paper.

Student A: Yes, professor. Dr. Green's study focuses primarily on the melting rate of polar ice caps.

Professor: Indeed. And which area did he find was warming most dramatically?

Student A: According to the charts, the Arctic circle is warming at more than twice the global average.

Professor: Correct. We also want to examine greenhouse gas footprints. While carbon dioxide is widely discussed, today we're analyzing methane emissions in detail.

Student B: Dr. Green's model suggests that emissions originating from agricultural soils have been severely underestimated in past carbon balance models.

Professor: Excellent point. Now, what policy solutions did the student panel advocate during the climate congress?

Student B: The majority strongly favored implementing carbon taxation as the single most effective tool to curb industrial output.

Professor: Interesting. Some countries are leading the way. For instance, Denmark has successfully reduced its industrial emissions by a full twenty percent over the past decade.

Student A: However, transitioning to renewables is hard. A key limitation of solar energy is the low storage capacity of modern batteries during peak winter nights.

Professor: Exactly. In addition, ocean warming has dramatic effects. Increased acidity is actively weakening the calcium shells of critical marine shellfish species.

We must remember that global oceans absorb a staggering ninety percent of excess heat generated by human industrial activities.

For your seminar essay due next week, please research the concrete ecological benefits of urban reforestation programs.

questionGroups.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.instruction: What is the primary subject of Dr. Green's newly published research paper?
options: A. The melting rate of polar ice caps
B. Solar panel efficiency in rainy regions
C. Deforestation rates in the Amazon basin
questions.explanation: Đoạn ghi âm cho biết: 'Dr. Green's study focuses primarily on the melting rate of polar ice caps.' Meo/Trap: khong chon theo keyword nghe thay dau tien. Hay nghe tiep cac correction, contrast va y cuoi cung cua speaker; sau do doi chieu spelling, word form, so it/so nhieu va word limit.

sections.title: Monarch Butterfly Migration Lecture
sections.instructions: Answer the questions below based on Part 4 of the listening recording.
sections.transcript:
Lecturer: Welcome back everyone. Today we are exploring one of nature's most incredible wonders: the epic migration of the Monarch butterfly. This tiny insect, weighing less than a gram, undertakes a journey spanning the entire North American continent.

As autumn approaches, millions of monarchs fly south to escape freezing winter temperatures. Their destination is a specific forest region in the high mountains of central Mexico.

Once they arrive, the butterflies cluster tightly together on the trunks and branches of oyamel trees. By packing together so densely, they preserve vital body heat and survive the cold winter nights.

When spring arrives, they wake up and begin their long return journey north towards the US and Canada.

Along the way, the female butterflies must search for a specific plant, the milkweed, which is the only plant they will lay their eggs on.

This is crucial because milkweed leaves contain mild toxins that make the caterpillars highly poisonous to birds and other predators. The distinctive orange and black warning colors of the monarch tell predators to stay away.

Interestingly, no single butterfly lives to complete the entire round trip. The journey requires up to four generations. This indicates that their path is determined entirely by genetic programming.

They navigate across thousands of miles using an internal solar compass combined with the Earth's magnetic fields.

Tragically, monarch numbers have plummeted. A major factor is habitat loss caused by expanding urban developments and logging.

Furthermore, widespread herbicide use on farms has wiped out vast amounts of wild milkweed plants.

To combat this, conservationists are asking communities to plant milkweed gardens to create supportive local habitats.

Finally, scientists track this migration by attaching extremely light, small sticky paper tags to the wings of individual butterflies.
